Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • US 82nd Airborne Division Ordered to Deploy to Middle East - Reports Sputnik 24 Mar 2026 -- Commander of the 82nd Airborne Division Maj. Gen. Brandon Tegtmeier, and members of his headquarters staff have received orders to deploy to the Middle East, Fox News reported on Tuesday.

  • Iran's air defenses down US cruise missiles near Tehran, over Markazi Province Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- Iran's integrated air defense network has successfully intercepted and destroyed multiple advanced US cruise missiles in the skies near Tehran as well as the airspace over the Markazi Province.
  • True Promise 4: Iran and resistance axis ops. against US-Israeli assets on Mar. 24 Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- On Tuesday, March 24, 2026, the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) and the Iranian Army conducted multiple operations as part of Operation True Promise 4, which was launched immediately after the US-Israeli coalition carried out an unprovoked act of aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran on February 28.
  • Iran trained in 'asymmetrical warfare' for two decades waiting for US troops: Defense official Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- A senior Iranian defense official says the armed forces have "trained in asymmetrical warfare" for two decades in anticipation of American troops being deployed to "designated regions."
  • IRGC Navy orders container ship to leave Strait of Hormuz for lacking permit Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- The Navy of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has ordered a container vessel to turn away after it attempted to transit the Strait of Hormuz without obtaining the required authorization.
  • IRGC Ground Force vows crushing response to any aggression Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- The commander of the Ground Force of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has issued a stark warning to the United States and Israel, saying any threat or act of aggression will be met with a crushing response.
  • Iranian drone strikes hit Israel's key aerospace industries Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Army says its drone fleet has delivered precision strikes on Israel's key aerospace and weapons manufacturing industries.
  • Iran's armed forces determined to fight until complete victory: Commander Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- The commander of the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters says Iran's armed forces remain steadfast on the path to complete victory.
  • Op. True Promise 4: Iranian missiles rain down on Israel's intelligence hubs in new wave Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- In a fresh wave of retaliatory strikes, the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) targeted the Israeli regime's "secure" intelligence facilities in Tel Aviv.
  • 82,417 civilian units damaged by Israeli-US aggression: IRCS Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- The head of the Iranian Red Crescent Society (IRCS) says that the Israeli-US aggression has damaged more than 82,000 civilian units across the country.
  • Over 600 People Killed in Tehran as Result of Attacks by US, Israel - Authorities Sputnik 24 Mar 2026 -- More than 600 people have died in Tehran since the start of the conflict with Israel and the United States, Mohammad Ismail Tavakkoli, head of the Tehran district's emergency services, said.

  • Trump Says US 'In Negotiations Right Now' On Ending War Despite Iranian Denials RFE/RL 24 Mar 2026 -- President Donald Trump said the United States is "talking to the right people in Iran" about a deal to end the war, while Tehran launched attacks on Israel and Kuwait and US media outlets reported that thousands more troops may be headed to the region.
  • A Breakthrough Or Buying Time? Trump's Claim Of Talks With Iran Raises Questions RFE/RL 24 Mar 2026 -- President Donald Trump's claim that the United States has held talks with Iran has raised hopes of an end to the weekslong war rocking the Middle East.
  • Trump Claims US Talking With 'Right People' in Iran, Says Iranians Want Deal 'Badly' Sputnik 24 Mar 2026 -- The United States is having conversations with the "right people" in Iran, US President Donald Trump claimed on Tuesday, adding that Iranians are "badly" after a deal.
  • White House Says Situation Around US-Iran Talks 'Fluid' Sputnik 24 Mar 2026 -- The situation around the US-Iran talks is "fluid," with no speculations about possible meetings as part of these arrangements advisable until an official announcement is made, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said.
  • Trump suggests joint control of Hormuz with Iran People's Daily 24 Mar 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump on Monday said Washington had "major points of agreement" with Iran during the weekend talks that proceeded late into the evening Sunday and suggested the Strait of Hormuz could be "jointly controlled."
  • Trump says U.S. delays strikes on Iran's energy sites for 5 days People's Daily 24 Mar 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday that he had ordered the military to delay strikes on Iranian power plants and energy facilities for five days after what he called "productive" talks with Iran.
  • Trump says U.S. intent on reaching deal with Iran People's Daily 24 Mar 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday the United States is "very intent on making a deal with Iran," CNBC reported.
  • Trump claims "major points of agreement" with Iran while Tehran denies any contact People's Daily 24 Mar 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday that Washington had reached "major points of agreement" with Iran and was in contact with "a top person," even as Tehran denied any direct or indirect communication with the United States.
  • Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum raises stakes as Iran warns of retaliation People's Daily 24 Mar 2026 -- A sharp escalation in the Iran-U.S.-Israel conflict is unfolding as U.S. President Donald Trump on Saturday issued a 48-hour ultimatum demanding Iran fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz, warning of sweeping strikes on the country's power infrastructure, while Tehran signaled it would retaliate across the region if attacked.

  • Iran vows to make US, Israel regret aggression; China urges attackers to end 'bullying' behavior Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- Iran pledges to sustain its defensive and retaliatory measures until prompting the United States and the Israeli regime to "regret" launching their latest bout of unprovoked aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran defends sovereignty, territorial integrity with full force: FM Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi underscores Iran's determination to safeguard its sovereignty in the face of ongoing unprovoked American-Israeli aggression targeting the country's soil.
  • Araghchi: Strait of Hormuz 'not closed' to those who respect Iran's 'navigational rights' Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi says ships are avoiding the Strait of Hormuz because the United States and its allies fail to respect Iran's trade and navigational rights.
  • Mohammad Baqer Zolqadr appointed new head of Iran's Supreme National Security Council Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- Veteran military commander and strategist Mohammad Baqer Zolqadr has been officially appointed as the new Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council (SNSC).
  • Profile: Mohammad Baqer Zolqadr, Iran's new top security official Press TV 24 Mar 2026
  • IRGC issues ultimatum to Israel: End genocide or face heavy strikes Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has issued a severe warning to the Israeli military, slamming it for committing widespread war crimes in Gaza and Lebanon.
  • Iran warns any strike on infrastructure to leave US forces 'paralyzed', sunk Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- A senior Iranian official has issued a stark warning to the United States, stating that any attack on the country's infrastructure would result in a devastating response that would paralyze the enemy and sink its forces in the Persian Gulf.
  • Iran FM: Hormuz Strait open to ships from nations not backing US-Israeli aggression Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi says that the Strait of Hormuz is only closed to vessels belonging to aggressor parties and their supporters, while ships from other countries face no issues transiting the strategic waterway after coordinating with Iranian authorities.
  • Iran's IRGC Says Ready to Attack Israeli Forces in Gaza Sputnik 24 Mar 2026 -- Iranian armed forces will attack Israeli troops in the Gaza Strip if Israel continues its attacks on Lebanon and Palestinian territories, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said on Tuesday.
  • Israeli shelters 'legitimate targets' if Iran's residential areas attacked: IRGC Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps' (IRGC) Intelligence Unit says Iran will consider the Israeli regime's shelters "legitimate targets" if residential areas in the country come under attack.
  • Pezeshkian: Insecurity in Strait of Hormuz direct result of US-Israeli aggression Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- Iran's President Maosud Pezeshkian says the insecurity in the Strait of Hormuz is the direct result of US-Israeli aggression against Iran, asserting that Tehran has taken necessary measures to ensure safe passage for non-hostile vessels through the strategic waterway.

  • US-Israeli Attack on Iran Torpedoes Years of Nuclear Diplomacy Efforts - Russian Official Sputnik 24 Mar 2026 -- The unprovoked aggression by the United States and Israel against Iran has nullified years of efforts to resolve the situation around Iran's nuclear program, Russian Security Council Secretary Sergei Shoigu said on Tuesday.

  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Drone Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 24 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Drone Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 24 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Drone Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 24 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Drone Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 24 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Two Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 24 Mar 2026
  • PM call with Crown Prince of Saudi Arabia, His Royal Highness Mohammed bin Salman: 24 March 2026 Gov.UK 24 Mar 2026

  • Pakistan ready to host US-Iran talks — diplomat Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 24 Mar 2026 -- Islamabad is ready to host talks between Washington and Tehran if they agree, Spokesperson for the Pakistani Ministry of Foreign Affairs Tahir Hussain Andrabi, told CNN, according to TASS.
  • Pakistan Steps Forward As Future 'Host' In Iran Diplomacy RFE/RL 24 Mar 2026 -- Pakistan has offered to host talks aimed at bringing the Iran conflict to an end, highlighting the country's strengthened ties in both the Middle East and with Washington.
  • EU could deploy mission to Hormuz Strait after hostilities end — EC president Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 24 Mar 2026 -- The Europeans could send a mission to the Strait of Hormuz only after the US-Israeli war on Iran is over, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen confirmed following talks in Canberra with Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese, according to TASS.
  • "Although it is Israel's war, the price is being paid by the whole world" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 24 Mar 2026 -- Delivering remarks following the Presidential Cabinet Meeting, President Erdoğan, as regards the attacks on Iran, said: "The last 25 days have shown us that although it is Israel's war, the price is being paid by the whole world. It is Netanyahu's war to hold on to power, but it is the eight billion who are suffering. The gang of massacre, led by Netanyahu, must immediately be stopped in the name of regional peace and humanity, and all the countries must display a bold and proactive attitude to this end."
  • FM asks Iran to guarantee vessel safety in Strait of Hormuz KOREA.net 24 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Cho Hyun on March 23 in phone talks with Iranian Foreign Minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi pressed for safety guarantees for ships in the Strait of Hormuz.
  • Outcome of ROK-Oman Foreign Ministers' Telephone Conversation (March 24) ROK MFA 24 Mar 2026
  • Japan to release state oil stockpiles this week, 2nd time amid Mideast conflict Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 24 Mar 2026 -- Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said on Tuesday that Tokyo will start releasing oil from state reserves on Thursday, as concerns over supply disruptions escalate amid the ongoing US-Israeli war against Iran, Anadolu Agency reports citing local media.
  • Japan to release oil reserves to curb soaring crude prices, but domestic concerns mount over economic impact Global Times 24 Mar 2026 -- Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi announced on Tuesday that Japan will begin releasing emergency oil reserves on Thursday as the Japanese government attempts to curb surging energy prices due to the situation in the Middle East, according to Japanese media reports. Will such a move be effective in dealing with soaring prices? How has the Japanese public reacted to the government's response to the crisis?

  • Japan-Malaysia Summit Telephone Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 24 Mar 2026
  • Japan-Marshall Islands Summit Telephone meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 24 Mar 2026

  • Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi addresses the Rajya Sabha on the ongoing conflict in West Asia India PIB 24 Mar 2026 -- The Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi addressed the Rajya Sabha today, presenting the Government's position on the situation arising from the ongoing conflict in West Asia
  • English rendering of PM's remarks in the Rajya Sabha on the ongoing conflict in West Asia India PIB 24 Mar 2026
  • Sri Lanka issues fuel, energy conservation guidelines amid Mideast tensions People's Daily 24 Mar 2026 -- Sri Lanka's Office of the Commissioner General of Essential Services has issued a set of guidelines to government institutions on the prudent use of fuel and energy amid possible disruptions to fuel imports caused by escalating tensions in the Middle East.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • DoW Comptroller and IG Announce Refined Approach to Achieve FY28 Clean Audit Objective DoD 24 Mar 2026
  • Remarks by Under Secretary of War for Policy Elbridge Colby at the Ananta Centre in India (As Prepared) DoD 24 Mar 2026
  • US Army launches online marketplace to revolutionize drone acquisition US Army 24 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Army launched the Unmanned Aircraft Systems Marketplace on March 24 — a new digital storefront designed to get trusted, cutting-edge drone technology into the hands of Soldiers faster than ever before. This digital one-stop shop will allow Army units, government partners and allied nations to efficiently procure vetted UAS solutions.
  • Wyoming Guard Expands Field Artillery with Rocket Training Army News 24 Mar 2026 -- The Wyoming National Guard expanded its field artillery capability by training Soldiers March 20 in one of the Army's most in-demand roles, the 13M High Mobility Artillery Rocket System (HIMARS) crew member.
  • Hegseth Lauds Parity in Pay, Benefits for Guardsmen, Praises Memphis Crime Reduction DoD News 24 Mar 2026 -- During a roundtable review of crime reduction in the city of Memphis, Tennessee, yesterday, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth lauded President Donald J. Trump's announcement that some National Guardsmen currently deployed around the U.S. will soon receive deployment pay and benefits equal to those of active-duty troops.
  • Joint Interagency Task Force Awards Critical Counter-UAS Contract DoD News 24 Mar 2026 -- Joint Interagency Task Force 401 has successfully executed additional contract awards as part of Domestic Shield to procure counter-unmanned aerial systems capability in support of U.S. Northern Command and U.S. Strategic Command.
  • Air Force Leaders visit Minot AFB to advance Sentinel modernization US Air Force 24 Mar 2026 -- Gen. Dale R. White, Department of War direct reporting portfolio manager for critical major weapon systems, visited Minot Air Force Base, March 19-20, to assess mission requirements and ongoing preparations supporting the Sentinel intercontinental ballistic missile system modernization efforts.
  • U.S. Navy Opens New Expeditionary Maintenance Facility at Camp Mitchell US Navy 24 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Navy leaders, Sailors, and civilian partners marked the completion of a new expeditionary maintenance facility (EMF) during a ribbon-cutting ceremony at Camp Mitchell aboard Naval Station (NAVSTA) Rota, Spain, March 23, 2026.
  • US has 2 months of rare earths left to replenish weapons amid Iran war: Report Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- The United States has only two months' worth of rare earth elements critically important in the manufacturing of modern weaponry amid its joint aggression with the Israeli regime on Iran, a report says.

  • US Sees Opportunity for Improved Treaty in New START Expiration - State Department Sputnik 24 Mar 2026 -- The United States believes that the expiration of the New START (New Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty) provides an opportunity for reaching a better agreement, US Under Secretary of State for Arms Control and International Security Thomas DiNanno said on Tuesday.
  • US, Russia, China Could Jointly Police Global Nuclear Arsenal in Future - State Department Sputnik 24 Mar 2026 -- It is possible that the United States, Russia and China could jointly police the global arsenal of nuclear weapons in the future, US Under Secretary of State for Arms Control and International Security Thomas DiNanno said on Tuesday.

  • NATO gathers experts for Integrated Air and Missile Defence Conference in Poland NATO 24 Mar 2026 -- The fifth annual NATO Integrated Air and Missile Defence (IAMD) Conference was held in Gdynia, Poland, from 9 to 11 March 2026, serving as a key forum for high-level policy discussions on the current security environment and the future direction of NATO IAMD.

  • Deputy Minister of Defence of the Russian Federation Vasily Osmakov holds working meeting with Colonel General Pham Truong Son, Deputy Chief of the General Staff of the Vietnamese People's Army Russia MoD 24 Mar 2026 -- Deputy Minister of Defence of the Russian Federation Vasily Osmakov held a working meeting with the Deputy Chief of the General Staff of the Vietnamese People's Army, Colonel General Pham Truong Son.

  • NATO is interested in Ukraine's experience in counter-drone defense and in the exchange of military innovations Ukraine MOD 24 Mar 2026 -- Ukraine and NATO are expanding cooperation in counter-drone defense, the exchange of combat experience, and the development of innovative technologies. Ukraine stands ready to share its unique solutions derived from the lessons of modern warfare and integrate them into joint projects with partners.
  • Ukraine advances efforts to block russia's shadow fleet, which enables the aggressor to sustain the war Ukraine MOD 24 Mar 2026 -- Approximately 1,500 tankers currently make up russia's so-called shadow fleet, enabling the aggressor to sell oil while circumventing sanctions and channel the proceeds into the war against Ukraine.

  • Australia-European Union Security and Defence Partnership Australia DOD 24 Mar 2026 -- Australia and the European Union are strengthening cooperation through a new Security and Defence Partnership.
  • Global space chiefs unite in Canberra Australia DOD 24 Mar 2026 -- The inaugural Space Chief's Conclave brought together international space leaders for an inspiring day of connection, collaboration and forward-looking discussion.

  • RNZAF, Australian and US C-130J Hercules crews train together in South Island exercise New Zealand Defence Force 24 Mar 2026 -- C-130J Hercules crews from New Zealand, Australia and the United States are conducting precision air mobility training in the skies above the upper South Island over the coming days.

  • Belarusian Military Practicing Counter-Drone Tactics During Drills - Defense Ministry Sputnik 24 Mar 2026 -- As part of a readiness check, troops from the Belarusian army's Western Operational Command are conducting exercises at training grounds to practice fighting a mock enemy, with a focus on countering drones, the Ministry of Defense said on Tuesday.

  • Raksha Mantri reviews West Asia situation & its effect on India's defence preparedness India PIB 24 Mar 2026 -- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh took stock of the situation in West Asia and its effect on India's defence preparedness during a meeting with Chief of Defence Staff, Service Chiefs, Defence Secretary, Secretary (Defence Production) and Chairman, DRDO in New Delhi on March 24, 2026

  • President Lee calls self-reliance 'core of national defense' KOREA.net 24 Mar 2026 -- President Lee Jae Myung on March 23 said "a self-reliant national defense is the core of an integrated defense" at a meeting of a central integrated defense council at the state guesthouse Yeongbingwan of Cheong Wa Dae in Seoul.

  • New military diplomacy training to upskill specialists in negotiation and languages, as part of new Defence Diplomacy Strategy UK MOD 24 Mar 2026 -- UK military personnel will be put through their paces as part of a new diplomacy training program - upskilling specialists in negotiation and languages as part of the UK's first Defence Diplomacy Strategy.

  • Bilateral defence consultations with Germany Government of Iceland 24 Mar 2026 -- evelopments in security affairs, NATO's defence posture, and defence and security cooperation between Iceland and Germany, with a focus on North Atlantic security, where among the principal topics addressed during bilateral defence consultations held in Berlin last week.

  • Data on interceptions of aircraft completed near the Baltic States' borders on March 16 - 22, 2026 Lithuania MND 24 Mar 2026

  • Opening Ceremony of Multinational Exercise SEA SHIELD 2026 Romania - Ministry of National Defence 24 Mar 2026 -- The opening ceremony of Multinational Exercise SEA SHIELD 2026 took place on Monday, March 23rd, in Constanța, in the presence of the Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, and the Chief of Defence Staff, General Gheorghiță Vlad.

  • Colombian military plane crash kills at least 66 Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 24 Mar 2026 -- A Colombian Air Force plane crashed shortly after takeoff in the south of the country, leaving at least 66 people dead and dozens injured, BBC reports citing officials.

  • Navy frigate aces 'hardest task,' detects Indian submarine in exercise Philippine News Agency 24 Mar 2026 -- Philippine Navy (PN) guided missile frigate BRP Miguel Malvar (FFG-6), which took part in the 3rd Indian Navy International Fleet Review (IFR) and Multilateral Naval Exercise MILAN 2026 last month, successfully detected an Indian Navy submarine during the anti-submarine warfare (ASW) phase of the maneuvers.
  • Japan to send 'combat troops' for 2026 Balikatan Philippine News Agency 24 Mar 2026 -- Japan is sending "combat troops" to participate in the upcoming 2026 Balikatan exercises in the Philippines, Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) chief Gen. Romeo Brawner Jr. said Tuesday.
  • AFP chief: Japan key ally vs. emerging security issues Philippine News Agency 24 Mar 2026 -- Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) chief Gen. Romeo Brawner Jr. on Tuesday said the country's strong partnership with Japanese defense forces is helping it address emerging security challenges, especially in the West Philippine Sea.

  • Hezbollah fires over 30 rockets at Haifa, Nahariya in extensive barrage Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- Lebanon's Hezbollah resistance movement launched extensive rocket attacks on the Israeli-occupied cities of Haifa and Nahariya on Tuesday, firing multiple types of missiles in a series of operations that also targeted Israeli military positions along the border

  • Gaza: Commitment to US-backed plan crucial to recovery, Security Council hears UN News 24 Mar 2026 -- As tensions escalate in the Middle East, the international community must not lose sight of the situation in Gaza, an official with US President Donald Trump's Board of Peace across the shattered enclave said on Tuesday in his first appearance in the UN Security Council.

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Central America UNOCHA 24 Mar 2026

  • China's 'scanning' of PH warship not enough to trigger hostilities Philippine News Agency 24 Mar 2026 -- A ranking naval official said Tuesday that the Philippines does not have to respond violently to a Chinese warship's fire control radar (FCR) scanning of a Philippine Navy (PN) guided missile frigate at the Sabina Shoal in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) last March 7.

  • MOFA Spokesperson's Statement on Japan's Authorization of High School Textbooks (March 24, 2026) ROK MFA 24 Mar 2026

  • Airstrike Hits Iranian-Backed Iraqi Shiite Militia Military Base in Ramadi - Reports Sputnik 24 Mar 2026 -- An airstrike hit a military base of Iran-backed Iraqi Shiite militias, the Popular Mobilization Forces, in the city of Ramadi in the western Iraqi province of Anbar, Al Jazeera reported citing police.

  • US Citizen Dennis Coyle Freed After Over A Year In Taliban Captivity RFE/RL 24 Mar 2026 -- Afghanistan's Taliban government announced the release and Dennis Coyle, a US citizen held for more than a year, after his mother plead for a pardon on the occasion of Eid al-Fitr, which marks the end of the holy month of Ramadan.

  • Afghan-Pakistani border: UN experts urgently call for lasting peace OHCHR 24 Mar 2026 -- UN experts* today urged Pakistan and Afghanistan to declare a fresh ceasefire and reach a peace agreement following the eruption of fighting in late February and the collapse of an October 2025 ceasefire.

  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on Sudan UN Secretary-General 24 Mar 2026
  • Sudan: Hospital strike highlights surge in drone attacks on civilians UN News 24 Mar 2026 -- The death toll from a horrific attack on a hospital in Sudan's Darfur has risen further, amid a "sharp increase" in drone attacks against civilians this year, UN agencies said on Tuesday.
  • Sudan: Sharp increase in civilian deaths as a result of growing drone warfare OHCHR 24 Mar 2026 -- A sharp increase in the use of drones to conduct airstrikes this year in Sudan underlines the devastating impact of high-tech and relatively cheap weapons in populated areas. According to information received, over 500 civilians were killed in such strikes from 1 January to 15 March.

  • Urgent support needed as 33,000 Congolese refugees return home from Burundi in a month UNHCR 24 Mar 2026 -- This is a summary of what was said by Ali Mahamat, UNHCR Head of Sub-Office in Goma, Democratic Republic of the Congo - to whom quoted text may be attributed - at today's press briefing at the Palais des Nations in Geneva.

  • Haitian gangs expand reach as police are accused of 'summary executions' UN News 24 Mar 2026 -- Deadly gangs in Haiti are expanding their reach to include control over key sea and road routes as police in the beleaguered Caribbean island nation are being accused of using "unnecessary and disproportionate lethal force and summary executions."
  • Gangs expand reach in Haiti amid persistent deadly violence - UN Human Rights Office report OHCHR 24 Mar 2026 -- A new UN Human Rights Office report published on Tuesday details the human rights impacts of the expanding reach of gangs in Haiti, including their control over key sea and road routes, amid persistent deadly violence. The violence involves gangs, security forces, private security contractors, and self-defence groups.

News Reports

  • NSA and ASD's ACSC Release Joint Guidance on LEO SATCOM System Risks and Mitigations NSA / Central Security Service 24 Mar 2026 -- The National Security Agency (NSA) joins the Australian Signals Directorate's Australian Cyber Security Centre (ASD's ACSC) in collaboration with the Australian Space Agency and others in releasing the Cybersecurity Information Sheet (CSI), "Securing space: Cyber security for low earth orbit satellite communications."

  • House Intelligence Committee Votes to Release Transcripts from 2019 Hearing with Former ICIG Michael Atkinson US House of Representatives Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence 24 Mar 2026 -- Today, the House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence held a business meeting to consider multiple Committee actions. During the business meeting, the Committee voted in favor of releasing two transcripts from 2019 hearings with the former Intelligence Community Inspector General, Michael Atkinson.

  • Meeting with Kaliningrad Region Governor Alexei Besprozvannykh President of Russia 24 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held a working meeting with Kaliningrad Region Governor Alexei Besprozvannykh.
  • Telephone conversation with President of Uzbekistan Shavkat Mirziyoyev President of Russia 24 Mar 2026

  • Rapid Expansion of Conflict Space Causing Concern - Russia's Lavrov Sputnik 24 Mar 2026 -- The rapid expansion of conflict space, involving the entire regions, is causing concern, Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov said on Tuesday.
  • Bipartisan Backlash Grows As Trump Administration Expands Russian Sanctions Relief RFE/RL 24 Mar 2026 -- US lawmakers are raising concerns after the Trump administration took additional steps to allow more Russian oil shipments to reach global markets despite existing sanctions.
  • Tsahkna at meeting with Italian Foreign Minister: we must act jointly to deter the shadow fleet Estonia MFA 24 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna was on a visit to Rome today, 24 March, where discussions with Italy's Minister of Foreign Affairs Antonio Tajani focused on the latest developments in the Middle East, Russia's aggression against Ukraine and their impact on European security, as well as on deterring Russia's shadow fleet.
  • Will Iran War Give Green Light To Russia's Power Of Siberia-2 Pipeline? RFE/RL 24 Mar 2026 -- The war in Iran and the resulting energy shock could revive China's interest in a long-stalled pipeline to import Russian gas, analysts told RFE/RL, potentially reshaping Beijing's energy strategy.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on March 24, 2026 PRC MofA 24 Mar 2026
  • China urges EU to abandon zero-sum mentality, protectionist measures following EC President's China remarks Global Times 24 Mar 2026 -- A Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson on Tuesday urged the EU to abandon the zero-sum mentality, steer clear of protectionist measures, and view China's development in a rational and objective light.
  • China discovers massive rare-earth deposit in Sichuan Province Global Times 24 Mar 2026 -- China's Ministry of Natural Resources (MNR) has revealed new mine exploration results showing significant rare-earth reserves in Maoniuping, Mianning County, Southwest China's Sichuan Province, the China Central Television (CCTV) Finance Channel reported on Tuesday.
  • China's top diplomat meets British PM's national security adviser Global Times 24 Mar 2026 -- China's top diplomat Wang Yi met with Jonathan Powell, British prime minister's national security adviser, in Beijing on Monday, and the two sides exchanged views on the situation in Iran, the Ukraine crisis and other issues.

  • Taiwan urges Denmark to correct designation or face consequences CNA 24 Mar 2026 -- Taiwan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) on Tuesday urged the Danish government to correct its description of Taiwan on residence permits that implies Taiwan is part of the People's Republic of China (PRC) or face retaliatory measures.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 24 Mar 2026

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un's Policy Speech at First Session of 15th Supreme People's Assembly of DPRK KCNA 24 Mar 2026 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, made an important policy speech, titled, "On the Tasks Facing the Government of the Republic at the Present Stage of Socialist Construction", at the First Session of the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the DPRK on March 23.
  • Kim Jong-un Officially Declares South Korea Hostile State Sputnik 24 Mar 2026 -- In his keynote speech at the first session of North Korea's 15th Supreme People's Assembly, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un officially declared South Korea a hostile state.
  • Press Review KCNA 24 Mar 2026 -- Following are major news items and articles in leading newspapers of the DPRK on Tuesday:
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Receives Congratulations from Russian President KCNA 24 Mar 2026 -- Kim Jong Un , president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, received a congratulatory message from V. Putin, president of the Russian Federation, on March 23.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Meets and Encourages Newly-Appointed Cabinet Members and Deputies to SPA KCNA 24 Mar 2026 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, met and congratulated the Cabinet members, newly appointed at the First Session of the 15th Supreme People's Assembly, and the deputies to the 15th SPA at the Pyongyang Assembly Hall on March 23.
  • On Fulfillment of State Budget of DPRK for 2025 and Its State Budget for 2026 KCNA 24 Mar 2026 -- There was a report on the fulfillment of the state budget for 2025 and on the state budget for 2026 deliberated by the Budget Committee of the Supreme People's Assembly at the First Session of the 15th SPA.
  • Second-day Sitting of First Session of 15th SPA of DPRK Held KCNA 24 Mar 2026 -- The second-day sitting of the First Session of the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea was held on March 23.
  • Report on Cabinet's Work Submitted to First Session of 15th SPA of DPRK KCNA 24 Mar 2026 -- Deputy Pak Thae Song, premier of the Cabinet, made a report on the work of the Cabinet at the First Session of the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the DPRK.

  • Iran arrests over 90 operatives linked to 'Iran International' network amid Israeli-US war: Source Press TV 24 Mar 2026 -- Iranian security forces have arrested more than 90 individuals with ties to the "Iran International" network, knowledgeable sources told Press TV on Tuesday.
  • Spanish Government condemns recent executions of opposition figures in Iran Spain MFA 24 Mar 2026

  • Joint statement Prime Minister of Australia 24 Mar 2026
  • Joint Statement by Ursula von der Leyen, President of the European Commission and Anthony Albanese, Prime Minister of Australia European Commission 24 Mar 2026
  • Press conference - Parliament House, Canberra Prime Minister of Australia 24 Mar 2026
  • Australia-European Union Security and Defence Partnership Prime Minister of Australia 24 Mar 2026
  • Opening remarks - Parliament House, Canberra Prime Minister of Australia 24 Mar 2026
  • Australia: Remarks of High-Representative/Vice President Kaja Kallas following the virtual signing ceremony of the EU-Australia Security and Defence Partnership European External Action Service (EEAS) 24 Mar 2026
  • EU and Australia strengthen relations with Security and Defence Partnership and Trade Agreement European External Action Service (EEAS) 24 Mar 2026 -- The EU and Australia have today announced the adoption of a groundbreaking Security and Defence Partnership. They have also concluded negotiations for an ambitious and balanced free trade agreement (FTA) and agreed to launch formal negotiations for the association of Australia to Horizon Europe, the world's largest funding programme for research and innovation. With these steps, the EU and Australia are delivering mutually beneficial outcomes and further reinforcing their already close relations in a time of geopolitical uncertainty.
  • Speech by President von der Leyen at the Australian Parliament European Commission 24 Mar 2026
  • Statement by President von der Leyen at the joint press conference with Australian Prime Minister Albanese European Commission 24 Mar 2026
  • EU and Australia strengthen relations with Security and Defence Partnership and Trade Agreement European Commission 24 Mar 2026
  • Australia, EU sign free trade deal, agree to strengthen security, defense partnership Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 24 Mar 2026 -- Australia and the European Union on Tuesday signed a free trade agreement (FTA) worth AUD10 billion (about $7 billion), as well as a security and defense partnership agreement, Anadolu Agency reports.

  • Opening speech by Commissioner Kubilius at the second EU-African Union Dialogue on Space European External Action Service (EEAS) 24 Mar 2026

  • Belarus - Release of prisoners (24 march 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 24 Mar 2026

  • The Daily Press Briefing (03/24/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 24 Mar 2026

  • Chinese FM spokesperson on Japan's reported plan to downgrade description for China ties Global Times 24 Mar 2026 -- The root cause of the current situation facing China-Japan relations is Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's erroneous remarks on Taiwan, which have sparked outrage from the Chinese people and tested the red line of the post-war international order, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Tuesday when asked for comment that, according to a draft reviewed by Reuters, Japan will downgrade its description of ties with China from "one of the most important" to instead describing China as an important neighbor and the relationship as "strategic and mutually beneficial."

  • Speech by President António Costa at SciencesPo - Paris School of International Affairs EC/CEU 24 Mar 2026
  • UNHCR 95th Standing Committee - Statement by the EU and its Member States - Agenda item 3 (a) Regional updates: Americas European External Action Service (EEAS) 24 Mar 2026
  • UNHCR 95th Standing Committee - Statement by the EU and its Member States - Agenda item 3 (a) Regional updates: Asia and the Pacific European External Action Service (EEAS) 24 Mar 2026
  • UNHCR 95th Standing Committee - Statement by the EU and its Member States - Agenda item 3 (a) Regional updates: Middle East and North Africa European External Action Service (EEAS) 24 Mar 2026
  • UNHCR 95th Standing Committee - Statement by the EU and its Member States - Agenda item 3 Regional updates: Africa European External Action Service (EEAS) 24 Mar 2026
  • Ghana: Press release on the signing of a Security and Defence Partnership and handover of EPF military equipment European External Action Service (EEAS) 24 Mar 2026 -- The European Union (EU) and the Republic of Ghana today signed a Security and Defence Partnership in Accra, marking a significant step forward in deepening their dialogue and cooperation on peace, security and defence.
  • Ghana: Remarks of High Representative Kallas at the joint press conference with Vice President Opoku-Agyemang European External Action Service (EEAS) 24 Mar 2026

  • Ministry of Foreign Affairs Secretary General Vseviov met with the Secretary General of the French Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Paris Estonia MFA 24 Mar 2026

  • Finland becomes contributing partner in US-led programme promoting responsible use of small modular reactor technology Finnish Government 24 Mar 2026

  • UN Special Rapporteur expresses deep concern over announcements of pardons for members of security forces involved in human rights violations OHCHR 24 Mar 2026 -- The United Nations Special Rapporteur on the rights to freedom of peaceful assembly and of association, Gina Romero*, expressed alarm today at recent statements by Chile's incoming government regarding the possible granting of individual pardons to security force officials convicted of serious human rights violations during the 2019 social unrest.

  • PBBM declares state of nat'l energy emergency amid global supply risks Philippine News Agency 24 Mar 2026 -- President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. has declared a state of national emergency to address potential disruption in fuel supply and stabilize the country's energy sector.

  • Bypassing The Kremlin: Armenia And Azerbaijan Expand Trade, Testing Russia's Grip RFE/RL 24 Mar 2026 -- Longtime rivals Armenia and Azerbaijan are increasing trade in ways that analysts say could support peace-building, ease Armenia's longstanding blockade, and gradually weaken Russia's role in the region as Moscow remains preoccupied with its war in Ukraine.
  • Wider Europe Briefing: The EU's Plan To Defeat Russia In Armenia And Belarus After US Potash Sanctions Lifted RFE/RL 24 Mar 2026

  • Central Asia Emerges As Strategic Energy Player Amid Oil Crisis RFE/RL 24 Mar 2026 -- The global energy market is facing one of its most severe disruptions in decades with the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz - a vital artery for 20 percent of global maritime trade and a third of the world's liquefied natural gas (LNG) - amid the US-Israeli war with Iran.

  • Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan chairs Anti-Corruption Policy Council session Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 24 Mar 2026 -- Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan chaired the regular session of the Anti-Corruption Policy Council, which was attended by representatives of state bodies included in the council, as well as representatives of non-governmental organizations and invited participants.

  • World News in Brief: Airstrikes continue in Ukraine, refugees return to DR Congo, emergency aid for the 'Dry Corridor' UN News 24 Mar 2026 -- The latest wave of Russian attacks across Ukraine have reportedly killed and injured civilians and damaged critical infrastructure in Dnipro, Odesa, Poltava, Zaporizhzhia and other regions.
